text.title: Active Listening Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Generated script opens with "It sounds like you're frustrated..." before the speaker has said anything. Interviewee reads it as condescending and shuts down.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated reflective statements as openers; reflective statements are reactive, not generative.
antipattern.fix: Mark reflective_prompts as response-only in the script. Generate them as templates with FEELING + CONTENT slots to be filled live, not pre-baked.

antipattern.symptom: Generated question opens with "What" but embeds an assumption: "What problems did X cause?" forcing the speaker to accept the framing.
antipattern.root-cause: Open-opener test is purely lexical and misses semantic assumption-loading.
antipattern.fix: Run an assumption-extraction pass: any "What X did Y" reformatted to "Walk me through Y" or "Tell me about Y" with no presupposition.

antipattern.symptom: Team configures an agent to run a live customer interview using the RASA script. Agent cannot read tone, misses silence cues, output feels robotic.
antipattern.root-cause: Active listening is fundamentally reactive to real-time emotional signal; agents cannot adapt to body language or tone.
antipattern.fix: Restrict agent role to (a) pre-conversation script generation, (b) post-conversation transcript annotation. A human runs the live conversation.

antipattern.symptom: Script lists Receive, Appreciate, Ask but skips Summarize starters. Interviewer talks past misunderstandings; speakers repeat themselves.

root-cause.source: Center for Creative Leadership — RASA Framework

antipattern.root-cause: Author abridged RASA to "what feels productive" — Ask seems substantive, Summarize seems redundant.
antipattern.fix: Validator rejects scripts without ≥3 summarize_starters. Post-pass transcript annotator flags interviews where Summarize labels are absent.
